title: 埃及十字架之谜
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\27-埃及十字架之谜-埃勒里奎因.txt
summary:
Officer Wain arrives at the colonial-era house with injuries from a recent brawl, informing Jonah Lincoln that Sergeant Vane defeated a man described as a "giant." Following the confrontation, Jonah, Esam, and the constable proceed upstairs to conduct an official investigation in the room of Mrs. Brad. Appearing anxious and ill, Mrs. Brad is questioned by Esam and Wain regarding her whereabouts between 10:30 and 11:00 p.m. on the night her husband, Thomas, was killed.

The investigators reveal that Paul Roman claimed she was with him near the docks, which contradicts her initial claim of being at the theater. Under pressure, Mrs. Brad admits she slipped out of the theater during the first act, claiming to Helen that she felt unwell. She explains that she took a train to Pennsylvania Station, disembarked early, and hired a taxi near Bradwood to return home secretly. She confirms she met Roman at the docks around 10:30 p.m. but maintains she saw or heard nothing unusual.

Helen Brad enters the room and expresses disdain for her mother's weakness in confessing. Helen reveals that she and Jonah had noticed Mrs. Brad's suspicious behavior that night and that she had observed her mother lying in bed asleep before the body was discovered. When Ellery asks if she had suspected something for weeks, Helen confirms she had. After Mrs. Brad denies that her stepfather knew of the affair, District Attorney Esam ends the questioning. Later, while watching Independence Day fireworks on Long Island, Dr. Avery expresses disappointment to Ellery, noting that Ellery's detective skills have yet to produce a significant breakthrough.
